decision_self_rationale: All 8 reader workers post-AC020-concurrent-body show cache_used_post_pct_of_limit = 3.35% (well below the 50% SKIP threshold) AND delta_miss_rate ranging 0.0230%-0.0237% (well below the 5% SKIP threshold). Both SKIP criteria are met cleanly across every worker — there is no cache pressure on the F.0 reader-worker page caches under the AC-020 hybrid-retrieval workload. G.4 (per-worker reader PRAGMA cache_size sweep) is not justified by the current evidence on this fixture and host.
host_caveat: Same worktree host as G.0 / G.1 (Linux 5.15-tegra, ~3x slower than the Pack 5 reference machine). G.3.5 is a screening step, not a perf packet — no AC-020 N=5 capture; absolute counts (~4250 hits / ~1 miss per worker per concurrent body) are not directly cross-host comparable but the ratios are.

red_tests_written.name: g3_5_cache_pressure_telemetry
red_tests_written.red_failure_mode: Compile-time: method `cache_status_per_worker_for_test` does not exist on `fathomdb_engine::Engine` (E0599) on the G.3.5-prompt tip d9adf23. Verified by `git grep cache_status_per_worker_for_test d9adf23 -- src/` returning no hits. The test was authored first to exercise the new debug-only `ReaderRequest::CacheStatus` broadcast; once the helper exists, the test would also panic at runtime if the cache_status reply count != worker_count (assertions on lines `assert_eq!(pre.len(), worker_count)` / `assert_eq!(post.len(), worker_count)`).

cache_size_limit_source: SQLite default cache_size = -2000 (KiB) = 2 MiB per connection. No `PRAGMA cache_size` override is present on the F.0 reader open path — only `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` and `PRAGMA query_only=ON` run after `Connection::open`, and the per-connection lookaside is configured via `sqlite3_db_config` (which does not affect cache_size). Pack 5 §5 do-not-retry list bars touching the production cache_size in this packet, and G.3.5 measures only.
decision_matrix_evaluation: Row 1 (SKIP_G4) matches: all 8 workers have cache_used_post_pct_of_limit = 0.033531 (3.35%) which is < 50%, AND all 8 workers have delta_miss_rate in [0.0230%, 0.0237%] which is < 5%. Row 2 (FULL_G4_SWEEP) does not match: no worker hits >= 90% cache used or > 15% miss rate; not even close. Row 3 (DISPATCH_BIAS_FLAG) does not match — see `dispatch_bias_evidence` below: max/min ratio for delta_hit is 1.032x (4354 / 4219) < 2x; delta_miss is uniformly 1 across workers; cache_used_post_bytes is uniformly 70320 across workers. Verdict: SKIP_G4, no bias flag.

dispatch_bias_evidence.verdict: round-robin dispatch is uniform within ~3.2% across workers on this AC-020 concurrent body; well under the 2x bias threshold. Page-cache footprint is bit-identical across workers (every reader sees the same query mix and the fixture is small).

ac017_runs.notes: `cargo test --release -p fathomdb-engine --test perf_gates -- ac_017 ac_018 --nocapture`. `test ac_017_vector_projection_freshness_p99_le_five_seconds ... ok`. Total wall: 14.70 s for the perf_gates test binary.

ac018_runs.notes: release profile; `AC018_NUMBERS drain_ms=80`. Well under the 2 s gate. Slightly faster than G.1's 95 ms; consistent with run-to-run host jitter on this Linux 5.15-tegra worktree.

g3_5_diagnostic_runs.command: G3_5_OUTPUT_PATH=/tmp/g3_5_evidence.json cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --test perf_gates -- --ignored g3_5_cache_pressure_telemetry --nocapture
g3_5_diagnostic_runs.notes: Test is `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]`-gated because the underlying `cache_status_per_worker_for_test` accessor follows the F.0 / G.1 `#[cfg(debug_assertions)] #[doc(hidden)]` convention. Spec §4.4 suggested `cargo test --release`, but that compiles out the helper; G.1's reader_pool tests ran in dev mode for the same reason. AC-017 / AC-018 still ran in release per §4.3.

unexpected_observations: Per-worker page-cache footprint is BIT-IDENTICAL across all 8 workers post-AC020 (70320 bytes each). This is a fingerprint of the small AC-020 fixture: 4 seeded docs + 4 distinct queries means each thread-affine connection materializes the exact same set of b-tree / vec0 / fts5 pages into its private page cache. On a larger fixture this uniformity would relax. For the G.3.5 SKIP_G4 verdict it tightens the case: there is no worker outlier hiding behind the median.

Hit:miss ratio is ~4300:1 on the concurrent body — the F.0 reader page caches are running in steady-state happy-path mode after the 16-search warmup. The single residual miss per worker per concurrent body is plausibly the first vec0/fts5 page that escapes the warmup query set. In other words the residual AC-020 gap on this host is NOT a page-cache-thrashing problem; it is something else (most likely the SQLite pcache1 global mutex itself, the WAL shared-memory atomics, or the parse_compile floor — all already on the orchestrator's G-phase candidate list).

delta_used_bytes (post - pre) is +4352 bytes uniformly per worker. That is exactly +1 SQLite default page (4 KiB) plus a small per-connection bookkeeping delta. A second page would only be materialized under cache pressure. The 2 MiB per-connection cache is thus carrying the AC-020 working set with ~30x headroom on this fixture.

Because cache_size is the canonical SQLite default (-2000 KiB), the 50% / 90% thresholds in §2 are evaluated against 2 MiB. If the orchestrator wants to verify the assumption empirically rather than assume it, a one-line `PRAGMA cache_size` readback in the same broadcast would suffice; G.3.5 deliberately did not add that to keep the diff minimal and inside the 'measure-only' mandate.

if_skip_g4.next_action: Defer G.4 entirely on this fixture and host. The page-cache evidence does not justify the run cost. Reassess G.2 (per-worker prepared-statement cache, sticky-connection revisit of E.1) per Pack 6.G handoff §4 default ordering — parse_compile = 22.52% conc from G.0 telemetry is the next-highest-share canonical-SQLite-amenable residual. If G.2 also lands INCONCLUSIVE / KEEP-but-no-AC-020-closure, the §9 packet-close path applies: ship 0.6.0 with REQ-020 deferred, open Pack 7 with the cumulative G-phase evidence including G.3.5's SKIP_G4 verdict as the bound on per-connection page-cache contribution.
if_skip_g4.rationale: Both SKIP thresholds are met by ~15x margin (3.35% vs 50% used; 0.023% vs 5% miss). A bigger reader cache cannot help when the existing one has 96.65% headroom and the steady-state miss rate is 1 page per 4300 hits.
if_skip_g4.rejected_alternative_PROBE_G4_MINUS_8000: Not selected. PROBE would be defensible if any worker were near a 50% cache_used or 5% miss-rate threshold; none are. Spending the worktree-spawn cost on a single sweep point that the data already says will be flat is not a positive-EV step.

if_full_g4_sweep.next_action: Not applicable to this evidence. Recorded for completeness: would have spawned G.4 with the full {-2000, -8000, -32000} KiB grid.

data_for_pivot.for_orchestrator: Codex `gpt-5.4` high reviewer optional per Pack 6.G §7 (G.3.5 is a screening pass, not a KEEP/REVERT lever — it adds debug-only diagnostic plumbing and a single `#[ignore]`-gated test). If the orchestrator wants reviewer cover anyway, the diff is small and the SKIP_G4 verdict is supported entirely by the 8 numbers in `per_worker_telemetry`.

next_phase_input.for_orchestrator: Apply the §8 stop rule SKIP_G4 branch: defer G.4 (per-worker reader PRAGMA cache_size sweep) and reassess G.2 (per-worker prepared-statement cache, sticky-connection revisit of E.1) per Pack 6.G handoff §4 default ordering. The G.3.5 evidence bounds the per-connection page-cache contribution to AC-020 at <= ~0.023% miss / <= 3.35% used — i.e. there is essentially no headroom for a bigger reader cache to recover. The remaining canonical-SQLite levers worth a worktree spawn are G.2 (parse_compile floor, 22.52% conc share from G.0) and / or G.3 (WAL checkpoint policy, only if G.0 evidence supports a write-side residual; G.0 reported checkpoint cost is small on the read-heavy fixture).
next_phase_input.for_packet_close: G.3.5 itself is a screening step; it does not move AC-020 numbers. Cumulative-vs-F.0 speedup is unchanged from the G.1 INCONCLUSIVE state (~3.53x median on this host vs the 5.33x KEEP target). Per Pack 6.G §9, packet success is 'every canonical-SQLite lever tried, KEEPs landed, residual gap documented for Pack 7' — G.3.5 contributes the page-cache leg of that documentation by ruling out the per-connection cache_size lever as a closer.
